name: Peña Redonda Ascent Trail
name_original: Peña Redonda desde Castrejón de la Peña — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: Hike the difficult Peña Redonda Ascent Trail in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, covering 8.6 miles with 2694 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Castrejón De La Peña offers diverse opportunities for outdoor enthusiasts within the Montaña Palentina region of Palencia, Spain. The area is characterized by its mountainous terrain, serving as the "first step" of the Palentina Mountain range, with prominent features like the Sierra de la Peña and distinctive limestone formations. Hikers can explore ancient yew and beech forests, such as the Tejeda de Tosande, and enjoy routes around the Embalse de Resgua, all set within a landscape of sculpted needles, cliffs, and valleys. This region provides a variety of trails suitable for different levels of hiking expertise. Best hiking trails around Castrejón De La Peña The most popular hiking route is Tejeda de Tosande Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This path explores one of the most important ancient yew forests on the Iberian Peninsula. Another top favourite among local hikers is On the shores of the Resgua reservoir — circular through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the reservoir within the natural park. Local hikers also love the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to a notable landmark, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Castrejón De La Peña is defined by mountainous terrain, ancient yew and beech forests, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Castrejón De La Peña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Castrejón De La Peña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castrejón De La Peña?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in Castrejón De La Peña, catering to various skill levels. This includes 8 easy routes, 23 moderate trails, and 3 challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, Castrejón De La Peña offers several easy routes suitable for families or beginners. These trails often explore the wooded hillsides and small lagoons, providing gentle walks through nature. For example, the region features routes that are less strenuous, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Castrejón De La Peña?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093088872/on-the-shores-of-the-resgua-reservoir-circular-through-the-montana-palentina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Resgua reservoir — circular through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful views along the reservoir and takes about 2 hours and 45 minutes to complete. Another circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103889160/respenda-de-la-pena-and-its-oak-and-fir-forests-circular-to-the-banks-of-the-valdavia-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Respenda de la Peña and its oak and fir forests — circular to the banks of the Valdavia river&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers in Castrejón De La Peña can experience diverse natural landscapes. You'll find mountainous terrain, ancient yew and beech forests like the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejeda de Tosande Yew Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and routes around reservoirs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesga Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Montaña Palentina Natural Park also features sculpted landscapes of needles, cliffs, and valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, the region offers challenging ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068363176/pena-redonda-ascent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda Ascent Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable difficult route, leading through mountainous terrain to the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2038327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda Summit&lt;/a&gt;, often taking around 5 hours to complete.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover significant landmarks. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejeda de Tosande Yew Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a natural monument. You can also hike to the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2038327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The region also features cultural routes that pass through villages with historical Gothic and Romanesque art.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Castrejón De La Peña?
A: The hiking routes in Castrejón De La Peña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from ancient forests to mountainous ascents, and the well-maintained paths that allow for varied outdoor experiences.

Q: Is there wildlife to observe while hiking in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, which encompasses Castrejón De La Peña, is home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for species such as brown bears, wolves, and chamois, especially in the more remote areas of the park.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many trails in the mountainous terrain of Castrejón De La Peña provide excellent viewpoints. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2038327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive vistas. Additionally, areas around the reservoirs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6168423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Camporredondo Reservoir from Mirador de Alba de los Cardaños&lt;/a&gt;, provide scenic outlooks over the water and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castrejón De La Peña?
A: The region offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer is also suitable, especially for higher altitude trails, while winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for snow and ice in mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any short walks available for a quick outdoor experience?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter outdoor experience, Castrejón De La Peña has options. Many of the easy trails are relatively short, allowing for quick walks through the natural surroundings without committing to a full-day hike. These are perfect for a brief immersion in nature.

Q: What kind of forests can I explore on the hikes?
A: The region is rich in diverse forest types. You can hike through ancient yew and beech forests, most notably the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068333744/tejeda-de-tosande-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejeda de Tosande Trail&lt;/a&gt; which explores one of the most important yew forests on the Iberian Peninsula. You'll also find holm oak, oak, and Pyrenean oak woods within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.
tips: The Peña Redonda peak is a limestone mountain of the Sierra del Brezo (or Sierra de la Peña) in the Montaña Palentina region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike usually takes around 5 hours and 5 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, pace, and how much time you spend enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Peña Redonda Ascent Trail is rated as difficult. It involves significant elevation gain and requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness. Hikers should be prepared for strenuous sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail is typically during late spring, summer, and early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the trails are free of snow. Winter hiking is possible but requires specialized gear and experience due to snow and ice.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, including the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail. However, always respect park regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail from Castrejón de la Peña are best confirmed locally. Typically, trailheads in natural parks have designated parking areas nearby. Public transport options to this specific starting point are limited, so driving is often the most practical way to reach it.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this trail is the Peña Redonda Summit, offering rewarding panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous landscape of the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. The trail itself passes through varied terrain, characteristic of high-mountain environments.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and location in a natural park, expect a mix of rocky paths, potentially steep ascents, and uneven ground. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other established paths. You will find yourself on parts of the 'Subida a Peña Redonda' trail for significant portions of the ascent.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a difficult hike like this?
faqs.answer: For a difficult hike, essential items include plenty of water, high-energy snacks, sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layered clothing for changing weather, a map or GPS device, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be very helpful on steep sections.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Redonda Ascent Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and duration, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness and hiking experience. There are likely more moderate options available within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina for families or those new to hiking.
